outcome_measures: Barratt Impulsivity Scale (BIS); Generalized Anxiety Disorder-7 (GAD-7); Patient Health Questionnaire-9 (PHQ-9)
main_findings: Higher depressive symptom scores and increased attention impulsivity were observed following concussion.
Increased internalizing and externalizing behaviours, as well as overall mental health problems, were reported.

outcome_measures: Mental Composite Score (MCS); PHQ (depression module); CAGE questionnaire
main_findings: Individuals with >=3 concussions had lower MCS scores (<50) and higher rates of moderate to severe depression; no association was observed with alcohol dependence.
Concussion was associated with increased risk of mental health conditions, self-harm, and psychiatric hospitalization; no difference was observed in death by suicide.
Increased risk of mental health disorders, including mood disorders, psychosis, and substance use disorders; increased risk of ADHD in females; no association with suicide attempts.
Concussion was associated with higher odds of self-harm, depressive symptoms, and injury resulting from suicide attempts.
Greater concussion exposure was associated with increased symptoms of anxiety, depression, or both.
